text:
BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE — PATHOLOGY REPORT
Protocol Post-Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant Remission Assessment (Day +180)

================================================================================
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
================================================================================
Specimen ID:          SP-BMT-0180-DELGADO
Patient Name:         Marcus T. Delgado
Date of Birth:        [on file]
Medical Record #:     [on file]
Ordering Physician:   BMT Attending, Hematology/Oncology
Indication:           Post-allogeneic stem cell transplant protocol bone marrow
                      evaluation at approximately 6 months post-transplant;
                      assessment for remission status, chimerism, and minimal
                      residual disease in a patient with AML, FLT3-ITD
                      (high allelic ratio), ELN 2022 adverse risk, now in
                      morphologic CR1 following 7+3 induction (cytarabine /
                      daunorubicin) plus midostaurin; 10/10 MUD allo-HSCT
                      with FluBu4 conditioning.
Date of Procedure:    [to be added programmatically]
Date Received:        [to be added programmatically]
Date Reported:        [to be added programmatically]

================================================================================
TYPE OF SPECIMEN
================================================================================
1. Bilateral posterior iliac crest trephine core biopsies (right and left)
2. Bilateral posterior iliac crest bone marrow aspirate smears and clot section
3. Aspirate submitted in EDTA for flow cytometry (MRD panel, 10-color
   multiparameter flow cytometry)
4. Aspirate submitted for cytogenetic studies (G-band karyotype)
5. Aspirate submitted for molecular studies: FLT3-ITD PCR, DNMT3A R882H
   digital droplet PCR (ddPCR), STR-based donor/recipient chimerism
   (whole blood, CD3+ T-cell fraction, CD33+ myeloid fraction)

================================================================================
GROSS DESCRIPTION
================================================================================
Received are two formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded core biopsy specimens labeled
"Right posterior iliac crest" and "Left posterior iliac crest," respectively.

Right core biopsy: A cylindrical trephine core measuring 2.0 cm in length and
approximately 0.2 cm in diameter. The external surface is smooth and tan-pink.
The specimen is processed entirely in one cassette following decalcification.

Left core biopsy: A cylindrical trephine core measuring 1.9 cm in length and
approximately 0.2 cm in diameter. Appearance is similar to the right-sided
specimen. Processed entirely in one cassette following decalcification.

Aspirate smears: Ten Wright-Giemsa-stained smears of adequate quality with
particles visible on six of ten slides. Aspirate clot section: a 0.4 x 0.3 x
0.2 cm tan-red clot received fixed in formalin; processed in one cassette.

All material is adequate for evaluation.

================================================================================
DECALCIFICATION
================================================================================
Formic acid decalcification performed on core biopsies prior to processing.
Adequate decalcification confirmed; tissue sectioned without artifact.

================================================================================
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
================================================================================
CORE BIOPSY (Right and Left, concordant findings reported together):

Architecture: Both core biopsies demonstrate normocellular marrow, estimated at
50–60% cellularity, appropriate for the patient's age (48–49 years). Bone
trabecular architecture is intact and unremarkable. No osteoclastic or
osteoblastic activity of note. Paratrabecular regions show no abnormal
cell clustering.

Erythropoiesis: Erythroid islands are well-formed, present in moderate numbers,
and distributed normally throughout the intertrabecular spaces. Normoblasts of
all stages are identified (pronormoblasts, basophilic, polychromatophilic, and
orthochromatophilic normoblasts). Maturation is orderly with no dysplastic
changes. No megaloblastic changes.

Myelopoiesis: Myeloid precursors are present in normal proportions with
representation of all maturational stages from myeloblasts through mature
neutrophils. A myeloid-to-erythroid (M:E) ratio of approximately 2.5:1 is
estimated, within normal limits. Myeloblasts are inconspicuous; no abnormal
blast clusters or sheets are identified. Nuclear lobulation is appropriate
along the granulocytic series. No Pelger-Huet anomaly. No hypersegmentation.
No significant dysgranulopoiesis.

Megakaryopoiesis: Megakaryocytes are adequate in number, averaging 4–6 per
high-power field in the core. Megakaryocytes demonstrate polylobated nuclei
with appropriate cytoplasmic granularity. No hypolobated (monolobate or
bilobate) forms. No clustering along trabeculae. No emperipolesis.

Blasts: No morphologically identifiable blast aggregates or individually
cytologically atypical immature cells. Blast percentage by morphology is
estimated at less than 2%, within normal limits.

Stroma and vasculature: Sinusoidal architecture intact. No evidence of fibrosis
on routine H&E.

ASPIRATE SMEARS AND CLOT SECTION:

Particles and spicules are present and evaluable. Cellularity is adequate.
Differential count (500-cell differential on aspirate smears):
  - Myeloblasts: 1.2%
  - Promyelocytes: 2.4%
  - Myelocytes: 8.6%
  - Metamyelocytes: 9.2%
  - Bands/neutrophils: 28.4%
  - Lymphocytes: 18.2%
  - Monocytes: 3.8%
  - Eosinophils: 2.1%
  - Basophils: 0.4%
  - Erythroid precursors (all stages): 25.7%
M:E ratio: approximately 2.9:1 (normal).
No Auer rods identified.
No dysplastic erythroid forms (no nuclear budding, no internuclear bridging,
no ringed sideroblasts on iron stain).
No abnormal lymphoid infiltrates.
Plasma cells: less than 1%, within normal limits.
No granulomas identified.
No foreign material or storage cells.

RETICULIN STAIN (Silver impregnation, MF grading per WHO 2022):
Grade 0 fibrosis. No increase in reticulin fibers. Normal loose network pattern.

IRON STAIN (Prussian Blue):
Storage iron (hemosiderin in macrophages): present, graded 1+ (mildly decreased,
consistent with post-transplant state and prior cytotoxic therapy). No ringed
sideroblasts identified.

================================================================================
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
================================================================================
Performed on paraffin sections of right core biopsy. Controls adequate.

  CD34:      Highlights rare scattered progenitor cells, estimated 1–2%,
             with normal non-paratrabecular distribution. No CD34+ blast
             clusters or sheets identified. No interstitial or nodular
             aggregates of CD34+ cells.

  CD117 (c-Kit): Scattered mast cells and early myeloid precursors highlighted.
             No aberrant co-expression with CD34 suggestive of residual
             blasts. No atypical CD117+ cell aggregates.

  CD3:       Highlights a normal proportion of interspersed T lymphocytes,
             appropriate for post-transplant marrow with ongoing immune
             reconstitution.

  CD20:      Scattered B-lymphocytes present, no B-cell nodules or aggregates.
             Normal B-cell regeneration pattern.

  MPO (myeloperoxidase): Highlights maturing myeloid elements throughout,
             normal distribution from early precursors through mature neutrophils.
             No aberrant MPO expression.

  CD138:     Rare plasma cells, estimated less than 1%.

No aberrant antigen co-expression pattern suspicious for residual or recurrent
AML is identified on immunohistochemical evaluation.

================================================================================
ANCILLARY STUDIES
================================================================================
The following studies were performed on aspirate material and are reported
as follows:

--- CYTOGENETICS (Conventional G-band karyotype, aspirate) ---
Result: 46,XY [20/20 metaphases analyzed]
Interpretation: Normal male karyotype. No clonal cytogenetic abnormalities
detected. No structural or numerical chromosomal aberrations identified.
Note: Karyotype reflects donor genome; this finding is consistent with
complete donor myeloid engraftment as confirmed by chimerism studies below.

--- DONOR/RECIPIENT CHIMERISM (STR-based short tandem repeat analysis) ---
Material: Bone marrow aspirate fractionated by immunomagnetic selection
(whole marrow, CD3+ T-lymphocyte fraction, CD33+ myeloid fraction)

  Whole blood (unfractionated) chimerism:   99% donor / 1% recipient
  CD3+ T-lymphocyte fraction chimerism:     96% donor / 4% recipient
  CD33+ myeloid fraction chimerism:         100% donor / 0% recipient

Interpretation: Full donor chimerism in the myeloid compartment. High-level
donor chimerism in the T-lymphocyte compartment. Compared to day +30 assessment
(97% whole blood donor, 99% myeloid donor, 91% T-cell donor), there is
progression to near-complete/complete donor chimerism in all compartments.
No evidence of autologous reconstitution or mixed chimerism in the myeloid
lineage.

--- FLT3-ITD PCR (Qualitative, capillary electrophoresis-based) ---
Material: Bone marrow aspirate
Assay sensitivity: 1 mutant in 10,000 normal alleles (10^-4)
Result: NEGATIVE — FLT3-ITD not detected
Interpretation: No evidence of FLT3-ITD positive leukemic cells detected
at the sensitivity of this assay. This finding is consistent with MRD-negative
complete remission.

--- DNMT3A R882H DIGITAL DROPLET PCR (ddPCR) ---
Material: Bone marrow aspirate
Assay sensitivity: 0.01% VAF (1 mutant molecule per 10,000 wild-type molecules)
Result: NEGATIVE — DNMT3A p.R882H mutation not detected
Mutant droplets / total droplets: 0 / 14,832 (adequate total input)
Interpretation: No residual DNMT3A R882H-mutant clonal hematopoiesis detected
at the limit of ddPCR sensitivity. Sustained molecular remission of the
co-dominant diagnostic clone.

--- MULTIPARAMETER FLOW CYTOMETRY (MRD assessment, 10-color panel) ---
Material: Bone marrow aspirate in EDTA
Panel: CD45, CD34, CD117, CD13, CD33, HLA-DR, CD14, CD64, CD19, CD3, MPO
Sensitivity: 0.01% (1 leukemic cell per 10,000 viable mononuclear cells)
Total events acquired: 500,000 viable CD45+ mononuclear cells

Result: NEGATIVE for minimal residual disease (MRD)
  - Leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) not detected
  - Blasts (CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/HLA-DR+/MPO+): less than 0.01%
  - No aberrant CD34/CD117 co-expression pattern
  - No aberrant loss of HLA-DR on CD33+ myeloid cells
  - No abnormal light scatter populations

Interpretation: MRD-negative by 10-color multiparameter flow cytometry at a
sensitivity of 10^-4. Normal reconstitution of hematopoietic progenitor
populations without evidence of the diagnostic leukemia-associated
immunohenotype.

================================================================================
DIAGNOSIS
================================================================================
BILATERAL POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE
(Protocol Day +180, Post-Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant):

1. NORMOCELLULAR BONE MARROW WITH FULL TRILINEAGE HEMATOPOIESIS AND NO
   MORPHOLOGIC EVIDENCE OF RESIDUAL OR RECURRENT ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA.
   Blast count less than 2% by morphology (within normal limits).
   No Auer rods. No dysplasia. Reticulin fibrosis grade 0.

2. MINIMAL RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD): NEGATIVE by multiparameter flow cytometry
   (sensitivity 10^-4) and by FLT3-ITD qualitative PCR (sensitivity 10^-4).

3. DNMT3A R882H by ddPCR: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01% VAF).

4. DONOR CHIMERISM: Full (100%) donor myeloid chimerism; 96% donor T-cell
   chimerism; 99% whole blood donor chimerism. Consistent with complete
   donor engraftment, 6 months post-MUD allo-HSCT.

5. CYTOGENETICS: Normal male karyotype 46,XY [20/20], consistent with
   donor-derived hematopoiesis; no clonal cytogenetic abnormalities.

Summary: Findings are consistent with AML in COMPLETE REMISSION with
MRD NEGATIVITY at day +180 post-allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
Full donor chimerism achieved. No morphologic, immunophenotypic,
cytogenetic, or molecular evidence of residual or recurrent disease.

================================================================================
SIGNATURE BLOCK
================================================================================
Interpreting Pathologist: [Hematopathology Attending]
Department of Hematopathology
Transcribed and reviewed by: [Hematopathology Fellow]
Case reviewed and signed: [date to be added programmatically]

This report was generated after review of all stained sections, aspirate smears,
and ancillary study results. Appropriate clinical and ancillary correlation
is recommended as always.
================================================================================
END OF REPORT
================================================================================
